title: Provider-specific database clients should not sit behind a generic `DATABASE_URL
rationale: This is a reusable backend integration pattern: if the product promises provider-agnostic Postgres via env configuration, the runtime DB client must also be provider-agnostic. Otherwise auth and ingestion failures appear as misleading connection errors.
description: Provider-specific database clients should not sit behind a generic `DATABASE_URL` contract. The app was using Neon HTTP for all runtime databases, which broke Supabase with malformed URL translation. The runtime path now uses the standard `pg` driver through Drizzle, so Supabase, local Postgres, and plain Postgres URLs all follow the same code path.
decision_reason: Category: other. Impact: 8/10. Defer until higher-priority signals processed.
